contraindications.clinicalRationale: ARBs are associated with teratogenesis and major congenital malformations, predominantly cardiovascular and neurologic abnormalities, when used during pregnancy. First trimester exposure risks cannot be confidently refuted.
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Advise contraception in women who are receiving ACEi or ARB therapy and discontinue these agents in women who are considering pregnancy or who become pregnant

contraindications.clinicalRationale: ARBs can cause hyperkalemia. When hyperkalemia is uncontrolled despite medical interventions to reduce serum potassium levels, dose reduction or discontinuation is required to prevent dangerous potassium levels.
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Reduce the dose or discontinue ACEi or ARB therapy in the setting of either symptomatic hypotension or uncontrolled hyperkalemia despite the medical treatment outlined in Practice Point 1.2.5

contraindications.clinicalRationale: ARBs are potent medications that can cause hypotension. When hypotension becomes symptomatic, it indicates inadequate organ perfusion and requires dose reduction or discontinuation.
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Reduce the dose or discontinue ACEi or ARB therapy in the setting of either symptomatic hypotension or uncontrolled hyperkalemia despite the medical treatment outlined in Practice Point 1.2.5

contraindications.clinicalRationale: In patients with advanced CKD experiencing uremic symptoms, ARBs may be discontinued temporarily to reduce symptoms while preparing for kidney replacement therapy. The goal is to resolve hemodynamic reductions in eGFR.
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Reduce the dose or discontinue ACEi or ARB therapy in the setting of... or to reduce uremic symptoms while treating kidney failure (estimated glomerular filtration rate [eGFR] <15 ml/min per 1.73 m2)

conditionDescriptions: Acute rise in serum creatinine >30% within 4 weeks
contraindications.clinicalRationale: A rise in serum creatinine >30% within 4 weeks of ARB initiation or dose increase may indicate acute kidney injury, volume depletion, bilateral renal artery stenosis, or concurrent nephrotoxic drug use. Investigation and management of underlying causes is required.
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Continue ACEi or ARB therapy unless serum creatinine rises by more than 30% within 4 weeks following initiation of treatment or an increase in dose

conditionDescriptions: Combination with ACE inhibitor or direct renin inhibitor
contraindications.clinicalRationale: The combination of ARB with ACE inhibitor, or ARB with direct renin inhibitor, is potentially harmful. Only one agent should be used at a time to block the renin-angiotensin system to avoid excessive RAS blockade and associated risks.
contraindications.sourceSnippet: Use only one agent at a time to block the RAS. The combination of an ACEi with an ARB, or the combination of an ACEi or ARB with a direct renin inhibitor, is potentially harmful
